steps.title: Recognize the growth pattern
steps.content:
The problem states the investment increases by 0.69% of its value the previous year.

This means the value is multiplied by $(1 + 0.0069) = 1.0069$ each year.

After $t$ years:$$V(t) = V_0 \cdot (1.0069)^t$$A constant multiplier raised to successive powers defines an exponential function, not linear.

This eliminates choices B (decreasing linear) and D (increasing linear).

steps.title: Determine if the exponential is increasing or decreasing
steps.content: The base of the exponential is $1.0069 > 1$. When the base exceeds 1, repeatedly multiplying by this factor makes values grow larger each year. This is an increasing exponential function, which eliminates choice A (decreasing exponential). That matches choice C — C is correct.
